channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: April Dining Events
item.description: Get ready for a month full of great food, fun, and community highlighted by the always popular Late Night Breakfast on April 30th from 9pm- 11pm! To see all the events coming up in April click the link above. We hope to see you at one or all of these events!
item.author: Students

item.title: Bootcamp Prepares Students to Earn National Fellowship
item.description: Juniors and Seniors interested in 30+ STEM graduate degrees are encouraged to sign up for the NSF GRFP Bootcamp designed to help you craft strong applications for this three year fellowship that provides salary and tuition. Complete virtual self-paced modules which begin August 3rd. Email madige@rpi.edu to request a link to a recording of an information session and/or to sign up for the Bootcamp. Graduating seniors: please use an email that will be active in August. U.S citizen or P.R. only
item.author: Students

item.title: Returning Housing Application Preferences Closing!
item.description: The ability for returning students to list their roommate and housing preferences will close at 5:00pm on April 24th. Please make sure your roommate groups are verified in the housing portal or they will not be considered.
item.author: Students

item.title: Corpus Festival | April 23-25, 2026
item.description: A weekend of creative experiments and gatherings that attune us to how bodily forms and bodies in formation construct our world. How do we take part in society by physically showing up? We all play our part in shaping power, control, freedom, and mutual responsibility.
item.author: Community

item.title: Black Holes Ain’t So Black
item.description: Saturday / April 25, 2026 at 2:00 pm How does liberation feel in the body? How do structures of violence shape the spaces around our bodies and around our planet? Talk, Performance Thuto Durkac-Somo, Jonathan González & Mario Gooden
item.author: Community

item.title: Bring your curiosity — Corpus Festival continues at EMPAC!
item.description: Date: Saturday, April 25, 2026 (all day) - How do our bodies shape experience and make meaning? Join us for Corpus, a festive weekend of performances, installations, and talks by artists who challenge and expand how we move through society, space, and one another. Your Saturday at Corpus features an afternoon with writer-filmmaker Thuto Durkac-Somo, choreographer Jonathan González, and architect Mario Gooden. Lounge at the Yellow Lab Book Bar, and don’t miss nibia pastrana santiago in the…
item.author: Community

item.title: Center for Research Advancing Financial Technologies (CRAFT) Annual Conference
item.description: The Center for Research toward Advancing Financial Technologies (CRAFT) invites you to its annual conference meeting on Thursday, April 30, 8:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m. in the CBIS Auditorium. Registration is required; business attire is expected. CRAFT is an NSF-funded industry-university research center. The event features keynote talks and cutting-edge fintech research and practice presentations, hosted by RPI and Stevens Institute of Technology.
item.author: Community

item.title: Earth Week Farmers Market April 23rd
item.description: Join Rensselaer Dining on Thursday, April 23rd for a Earth Week Farmers Market at the Student Union Patio from 11:00 am - 2:00 pm. Support local, eat fresh, and celebrate our planet together.
item.author: Community

item.title: Additional Commencement Ticket Information
item.description: On Friday, April 24th, starting at 9:00 AM EDT, students who registered for Commencement can claim one additional ticket. Please note that there are only 500 tickets available due to the number of graduates and the venue’s capacity. To claim your additional ticket, log in to your Tassel account and return to the registration form to the “your tickets” page. If you have any questions, please reach out to commencement@rpi.edu.
item.author: Community

item.title: Queering RPI | Collecting Positive Queer Experiences on Campus
item.description: Queering RPI is a storytelling and community building project designed to highlight the experiences, memories, and perspectives of queer students, faculty, and staff at RPI. The goal is to show students that they are not alone, that their identities matter, and that there is space for them on campus. This project collects and shares anonymous anecdotes, reflections, and moments from queer members of the RPI community.
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Workshop: “Leading from Anywhere” April 30, 2026 - 8:30 AM - 11:00 AM
item.description: At Rensselaer, we maintain that “leadership” involves a learnable set of actions and behaviors that is available to everyone. Whether it be leading a particular project or initiative, assisting others in overcoming obstacles and capitalizing on opportunities, or catalyzing co-creation with colleagues, Rensselaer community members model leadership every day. In this workshop, participants will explore the tenants that comprise everyday leadership and discuss the behaviors that define leadership…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: 2026 Faculty and Staff Climate Survey Launched
item.description: The 2026 Faculty and Staff Climate Survey launched by email on April 2, 2026. Your honest and candid opinions about the environment at RPI and how it affects your ability to do you work and thrive, professionally and personally, are important. You will find a personal link to complete the survey in your email from President Schmidt. Nearly 50% of employees completed this survey in 2024 and so far, we have approximately 22% completed. Please submit the completely anonymous survey by April 30,…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Changes for Submitting a LMS Summer 2026 Course Requests
item.description:
Instructors - Please read the entire announcement, as there are changes in submitting a LMS request due to moving to Ultra Course View. You can find Ultra Videos and documentation on our LMS support site.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
